important_issues.title: Duplicate route create is mis-mapped to ErrActorAlreadySigned
important_issues.detail: `repository.MapPgError` maps most SQLSTATE 23505 cases to `ErrActorAlreadySigned`; `RouteAdminService.Create` does not pass a unique-constraint hint for `approval_routes_tenant_profile_key`. A duplicate `(tenant_id, profile_code)` route therefore surfaces as an unrelated signoff error.
important_issues.impact: Wrong domain error contract for route administration; weak API behavior and confusing operator diagnostics.

important_issues.title: ErrRouteInUse depends on brittle message parsing
important_issues.detail: `MapPgError` maps SQLSTATE `P0001` to `ErrRouteInUse` only when `pgErr.Message` contains `ErrRouteInUse`. This is fragile under message text edits/localization and not tied to a durable DB identifier.
important_issues.impact: Behavior can regress silently if trigger text changes.

important_issues.title: Cancel area read is unlocked (TOCTOU window)
important_issues.detail: `CancelService` reads `documents.area_code` without lock prior to authz decision. While document delete is constrained by FK from `approval_instances`, area metadata can change between authz check and document update unless the row is locked or area immutability is guaranteed elsewhere.
important_issues.impact: Potential authorization inconsistency under concurrent metadata updates.

minor_issues.title: Capability cache key is not actor/tenant scoped
minor_issues.detail: `WithCapCache` uses capability+area only. It is request-safe in normal usage, but context reuse across principals would be unsafe.

minor_issues.title: Tripwire currently validates capability name only
minor_issues.detail: `enforce_capability_asserted()` intentionally ignores asserted area tuple in this phase; acceptable per comment, but weaker than intended final model.

questions_answered.1_guc_tripwire_sound: Mostly sound for defense-in-depth: `set_config(..., true)` is transaction-local, and trigger reads via `current_setting(..., true)`, avoiding cross-transaction contamination. Main caveat: this is not a hard security boundary against arbitrary SQL running in the same transaction/session, and current trigger checks cap presence (not area tuple).
questions_answered.2_cap_cache_concurrent_safety: `capCache` is mutex-protected, so concurrent goroutine access is memory-safe. Scope is request-context based; in standard request-scoped context usage this is fine. Residual risk is accidental context reuse across different principals because cache key excludes actor/tenant.
questions_answered.3_cancel_doc_race: Concurrent document delete is effectively prevented by FK `approval_instances.document_v2_id -> documents(id)` while instance exists, so hard delete race is low. However, area read is unlocked and can drift before update; adding `FOR UPDATE` (or stronger invariant on area immutability) would close TOCTOU.
questions_answered.4_route_lock_ordering: `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on `approval_routes` is a correct serialization anchor against concurrent inserts referencing the route, because FK checks take key-share locks that conflict with route row update/delete locks. Ordering appears safe; no obvious deadlock cycle in the reviewed path.
questions_answered.5_bypass_scheduler_in_trigger: Yes. Migration 0142b trigger explicitly reads `metaldocs.bypass_authz`; token `scheduler` is accepted and audited to governance_events, so scheduler inserts can bypass cap assertion as designed.
questions_answered.6_tenant_area_skip_security: The `($2 = 'tenant' OR upa.area_code = $2)` predicate means tenant-scoped checks ignore area filter and require only role-capability membership in any active area row. This is acceptable only if such capabilities are intentionally tenant-global; otherwise it is over-broad.
questions_answered.9_err_route_in_use_robustness: Not robust enough currently. Mapping relies on substring match of `PgError.Message`. Prefer stable discrimination (dedicated SQLSTATE/code convention, or DB detail field/token contract) independent of message wording.
questions_answered.10_duplicate_route_error_mapping: Not mapped correctly today. Duplicate `(tenant_id, profile_code)` route uniqueness violations (`approval_routes_tenant_profile_key`, SQLSTATE 23505) are not translated to a route-domain duplicate sentinel and can surface as unrelated errors.

fixes_required.description: Add explicit mapping for `approval_routes_tenant_profile_key` (and introduce a dedicated sentinel like `ErrDuplicateRouteProfile`) instead of falling through to `ErrActorAlreadySigned`.
Replace message-substring detection for `ErrRouteInUse` with a stable identifier contract (e.g., dedicated SQLSTATE/token strategy agreed with trigger).
Lock document row when fetching `area_code` (e.g., `SELECT area_code ... FOR UPDATE`) or enforce area immutability invariant to prevent TOCTOU during cancel authz.
